article_meta.title: Christmas travelers left stranded as airports see mass flight cancelations

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Christmas travelers left stranded as airports see mass flight cancelations
SOURCE: Fox Business

ARTICLE:

Travel Published December 27, 2025 8:38am EST Christmas travelers left stranded as airports see mass flight cancelations, delays JFK tops misery map with 77 cancellations as New Jersey, New York declare emergencies Facebook Twitter Threads Comments Print Email Add Fox Business on Google By Rachel Wolf FOXBusiness close video FOX Business Flash top headlines for December 26 Check out what's clicking on FoxBusiness.com. A winter storm on the East Coast has left post-Christmas travelers in a ho-ho-horrible position as airports see mass cancellations and delays. The website FlightAware showed that as of 7:50 a.m. ET on Saturday, 892 flights within, into or out of the U.S. were delayed and 637 were canceled. The website's "Misery Map" shows New York area airports, such as John F. Kennedy International Airport, LaGuardia Airport and Newark Liberty International Airport, experiencing the brunt of the travel chaos. HOLIDAY TRAVELERS FACE FLIGHT DELAYS AS FAA RESTRICTS AIRSPACE NATIONWIDE AMID WINTER WEATHER: REPORT Travelers at LaGuardia Airport (LGA) in the Queens borough of New York on Friday, Dec. 26, 2025. (Michael Nagle/Bloomberg via Getty Images / Getty Images)New Jersey and parts of New York are under state of emergency announcements as over 50 million Americans are set to be impacted by the winter storm barreling through the northeast, Fox Weather reported. Officials in New Jersey, New York, Connecticut and Pennsylvania are urging travelers and residents to exercise caution during the storm, according to Fox Weather. Additionally, a flurry of winter weather alerts have been issued in areas from New York City to Philadelphia.As of 7:52 a.m. ET, JFK Airport, which topped FlightAware's Misery Map list, had seen 30 delays and 77 cancellations since 5:00 a.m. ET. The Federal Aviation Administration said that flights arriving at the airport had been delayed by, on average, two hours and 37 minutes.Saturday's delays and cancellations were preceded by similar travel chaos on Friday as the northeast braced for the storm. On Friday, a total of 8,816 flights within, into or out of the U.S. had been delayed and 1,710 had been canceled, according to FlightAware. A traveler looks at a departures board in Terminal B at LaGuardia Airport (LGA) in the Queens borough of New York on Friday, Dec. 26, 2025. (Michael Nagle/Bloomberg via Getty Images / Getty Images)SOUTHWEST OFFERING $67 FLIGHTS IN NOD TO VIRAL INTERNET MEME: 'TRENDY' SALE"I came to New York to have a 'Home Alone' Christmas just by myself," Danniel Sermone, who was stuck at JFK International Airport, told the New York Post on Friday. "I wanted to hang out. I went shopping, and went to Times Square, crossed up there for a while with the big crowd. My next available flight is at 6 a.m. on the 28th. So I have been hanging out at JFK for about eight hours and still got like 30 left." Travelers arrive to catch flights at O'Hare International Airport on what is expected to be the busiest day of the holiday season on Dec. 21, 2025, in Chicago. (Scott Olson/Getty Images / Getty Images)GET FOX BUSINESS ON THE GO BY CLICKING HEREThe west coast was not spared from the winter weather travel chaos as it experienced a disastrous rain storm, though it did not appear to cause the same number of disruptions. FlightAware showed that Los Angeles International Airport had seen 64 flight delays and 15 cancellations by 4:10 a.m. PST. Additionally, FlightAware showed that as of 4:10 a.m. PST Harry Reid International Airport had seen 16 delays and nine cancellations. U.S. Stock Market Quotes Arrives Weekly Get Our Newsletter Get a brief on the top business stories of the week, plus CEO interviews, market updates, tech and money news that matters to you. We've added you to our mailing list.
---

{
  "summary": "A powerful winter storm struck the East Coast after Christmas, causing widespread disruption for air travelers during one of the busiest travel periods of the year. As of Saturday morning, nearly 900 flights were delayed and over 600 canceled nationwide, with the New York metropolitan area—especially JFK, LaGuardia, and Newark airports—bearing the brunt of the chaos. The Federal Aviation Administration reported arrival delays at JFK averaging over two and a half hours. The situation followed similar travel turmoil on Friday, when nearly 9,000 flights were delayed and over 1,700 were canceled. States including New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, and Pennsylvania declared emergencies and issued weather advisories, urging residents and travelers to take precautions. Individual travelers, such as Danniel Sermone, shared accounts of being trapped in airports for extended periods due to limited available flights. While the West Coast faced significant rainfall, its main airports registered fewer flight disruptions, with Los Angeles International logging 64 delays and 15 cancellations by early Saturday. The broader impact is expected to affect over 50 million Americans as the storm moves through the northeast.",
  "key_facts": [
    "A major winter storm caused mass flight delays and cancellations after Christmas, heavily impacting East Coast travel.",
    "By Saturday morning, there were 892 flight delays and 637 cancellations nationwide, with New York area airports worst affected.",
    "States including New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, and Pennsylvania declared emergencies and urged caution.",
    "Friday saw nearly 9,000 flight delays and over 1,700 cancellations as travelers tried to navigate the busy holiday period.",
    "West Coast airports experienced fewer disruptions despite significant rainfall, with Los Angeles International recording some delays and cancellations."
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Fox Business",
    "Fox Weather",
    "FlightAware",
    "New York Post"
  ]
}

parsed_output.summary: A powerful winter storm struck the East Coast after Christmas, causing widespread disruption for air travelers during one of the busiest travel periods of the year. As of Saturday morning, nearly 900 flights were delayed and over 600 canceled nationwide, with the New York metropolitan area—especially JFK, LaGuardia, and Newark airports—bearing the brunt of the chaos. The Federal Aviation Administration reported arrival delays at JFK averaging over two and a half hours. The situation followed similar travel turmoil on Friday, when nearly 9,000 flights were delayed and over 1,700 were canceled. States including New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, and Pennsylvania declared emergencies and issued weather advisories, urging residents and travelers to take precautions. Individual travelers, such as Danniel Sermone, shared accounts of being trapped in airports for extended periods due to limited available flights. While the West Coast faced significant rainfall, its main airports registered fewer flight disruptions, with Los Angeles International logging 64 delays and 15 cancellations by early Saturday. The broader impact is expected to affect over 50 million Americans as the storm moves through the northeast.
key_facts: A major winter storm caused mass flight delays and cancellations after Christmas, heavily impacting East Coast travel.
By Saturday morning, there were 892 flight delays and 637 cancellations nationwide, with New York area airports worst affected.
States including New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, and Pennsylvania declared emergencies and urged caution.
Friday saw nearly 9,000 flight delays and over 1,700 cancellations as travelers tried to navigate the busy holiday period.
West Coast airports experienced fewer disruptions despite significant rainfall, with Los Angeles International recording some delays and cancellations.
